(1 comment)

Have you tested that this works?  I see also patch https://review.whamcloud.com/28496 is almost the same as this one.

Patch Set 5:

Alex, do you have a preference for this patch over https://review.whamcloud.com/28496 ?  If yes, then the commit message here should be improved, otherwise this patch could be abandoned.

Patch Set 5:

yes, I checked with the logs that we ptlrpc doesn't wake up for no reason. I'm fine with landing another patch.

parents.subject: LU-5170 lfs: Standardize error messages in lfs_setstripe()

commit.subject: LU-9660 ptlrpc: do not wakeup every second
commit.message:
LU-9660 ptlrpc: do not wakeup every second

Even if there are no RPC requests on the set, there is no need to
wake up every second. The thread is woken up when a request is added
to the set or when the STOP bit is set, so it is sufficient to only
wake up when there are requests on the set to worry about.

Change-Id: Iac01d8c46e8645ecb6303ce72e0f6c59f16dcd5d
Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
